1	/**
2	 * Integration tests: Course delete cascades to user courseEntitlements
3	 * Covers: cleanupOrphanEntitlements afterDelete hook on Courses collection
4	 *
5	 * #1288 — orphan entitlements surfaced as "Unknown" entries in the admin
6	 * dashboard course enrollment widget after a course was deleted.
7	 */
8	/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
9	import { afterAll, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
10	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
11	import { getPayload } from 'payload'
12	import { startMongoContainer, stopMongoContainer } from '@/infra/utils/test/mongodb-container'
13	
14	let payload: Payload
15	let originalDatabaseUrl: string | undefined
16	let tenantId: string
17	let categoryId: string
18	
19	beforeAll(async () => {
20	  originalDatabaseUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL
21	  // @ts-expect-error: TypeScript doesn't allow delete on process.env
22	  delete process.env.DATABASE_URL
23	
24	  const mongoUri = await startMongoContainer()
25	  process.env.DATABASE_URL = mongoUri
26	
27	  const config = await import('@payload-config')
28	  payload = await getPayload({ config: config.default })
29	
30	  const tenant = await payload.create({
31	    collection: 'tenants',
32	    data: { name: `ent-test-${Date.now()}`, slug: `ent-test-${Date.now()}` } as any,
33	    overrideAccess: true,
34	  })
35	  tenantId = tenant.id
36	
37	  const category = await payload.create({
38	    collection: 'categories',
39	    data: { title: 'Ent Category', slug: `ent-cat-${Date.now()}`, locale: 'he' } as any,
40	    overrideAccess: true,
41	  })
42	  categoryId = category.id
43	}, 120_000)
44	
45	afterAll(async () => {
46	  if (payload?.db?.destroy) await payload.db.destroy()
47	  await stopMongoContainer()
48	
49	  if (originalDatabaseUrl !== undefined) {
50	    process.env.DATABASE_URL = originalDatabaseUrl
51	  } else {
52	    // @ts-expect-error: TypeScript doesn't allow delete on process.env
53	    delete process.env.DATABASE_URL
54	  }
55	}, 120_000)
56	
57	const createCourse = async (label: string, title: string) =>
58	  payload.create({
59	    collection: 'courses',
60	    data: {
61	      courseLabel: label,
62	      title,
63	      categories: [categoryId],
64	      tenant: tenantId,
65	    } as any,
66	    overrideAccess: true,
67	  })
68	
69	const createUserWithEntitlements = async (email: string, courseIds: string[]): Promise<string> => {
70	  const user = await payload.create({
71	    collection: 'users',
72	    data: {
73	      email,
74	      password: 'test-password-1234',
75	      name: email,
76	      courseEntitlements: courseIds.map((id) => ({
77	        course: id,
78	        grantMethod: 'admin',
79	<<<<<<< HEAD
80	        transactionId: `txn-${id}`,
81	=======
82	        transactionId: 'test-transaction',
83	>>>>>>> origin/dev
84	      })),
85	    } as any,
86	    overrideAccess: true,
87	  })
88	  return user.id
89	}
90	
91	const getEntitlementCourseIds = async (userId: string): Promise<string[]> => {
92	  const u = (await payload.findByID({
93	    collection: 'users',
94	    id: userId,
95	    overrideAccess: true,
96	    depth: 0,
97	  })) as any
98	  return (u.courseEntitlements || []).map((e: any) =>
99	    typeof e.course === 'object' ? e.course?.id : e.course,
100	  )
101	}
102	
103	describe('Course delete cascade → orphan entitlement cleanup', () => {
104	  it('removes entitlement referencing the deleted course', async () => {
105	    const course = await createCourse('D1', 'Doomed Course')
106	    const userId = await createUserWithEntitlements(`user-d1-${Date.now()}@test.local`, [course.id])
107	
108	    await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: course.id, overrideAccess: true })
109	
110	    const courseIds = await getEntitlementCourseIds(userId)
111	    expect(courseIds).not.toContain(course.id)
112	    expect(courseIds).toHaveLength(0)
113	  })
114	
115	  it("leaves other users' entitlements untouched", async () => {
116	    const a = await createCourse('D2a', 'Course A')
117	    const b = await createCourse('D2b', 'Course B')
118	
119	    const affectedUserId = await createUserWithEntitlements(`user-aff-${Date.now()}@test.local`, [
120	      a.id,
121	      b.id,
122	    ])
123	    const unaffectedUserId = await createUserWithEntitlements(`user-un-${Date.now()}@test.local`, [
124	      b.id,
125	    ])
126	
127	    await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: a.id, overrideAccess: true })
128	
129	    const affected = await getEntitlementCourseIds(affectedUserId)
130	    expect(affected).toEqual([b.id])
131	
132	    const unaffected = await getEntitlementCourseIds(unaffectedUserId)
133	    expect(unaffected).toEqual([b.id])
134	  })
135	
136	  it('preserves grantMethod and grantedAt on retained entitlements', async () => {
137	    const keep = await createCourse('D3keep', 'Keeper')
138	    const drop = await createCourse('D3drop', 'Dropper')
139	
140	    const userId = await createUserWithEntitlements(`user-meta-${Date.now()}@test.local`, [
141	      keep.id,
142	      drop.id,
143	    ])
144	
145	    // Capture original metadata
146	    const before = (await payload.findByID({
147	      collection: 'users',
148	      id: userId,
149	      overrideAccess: true,
150	      depth: 0,
151	    })) as any
152	    const keptBefore = before.courseEntitlements.find((e: any) => {
153	      const cid = typeof e.course === 'object' ? e.course?.id : e.course
154	      return cid === keep.id
155	    })
156	
157	    await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: drop.id, overrideAccess: true })
158	
159	    const after = (await payload.findByID({
160	      collection: 'users',
161	      id: userId,
162	      overrideAccess: true,
163	      depth: 0,
164	    })) as any
165	    expect(after.courseEntitlements).toHaveLength(1)
166	    const keptAfter = after.courseEntitlements[0]
167	    expect(keptAfter.grantMethod).toBe(keptBefore.grantMethod)
168	    expect(keptAfter.grantedAt).toBe(keptBefore.grantedAt)
169	  })
170	
171	  it('no-ops when no users hold an entitlement to the deleted course', async () => {
172	    const course = await createCourse('D4', 'Unheld Course')
173	    // No users created for this course
174	    await expect(
175	      payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: course.id, overrideAccess: true }),
176	    ).resolves.toBeDefined()
177	  })
178	
179	  it('cleans up all orphan entitlements when > PAGE_SIZE users are affected', async () => {
180	    const course = await createCourse('D5', 'Popular Course')
181	    // PAGE_SIZE = 500 in the hook — create enough users to span at least 2 pages
182	    // (Keep COUNT low enough that the verification loops don't exhaust the test
183	    // MongoDB connection pool; batched $in queries keep it fast.)
184	    const COUNT = 100
185	    const userIds = await Promise.all(
186	      Array.from({ length: COUNT }, (_, i) =>
187	        createUserWithEntitlements(`user-d5-${i}-${Date.now()}@test.local`, [course.id]),
188	      ),
189	    )
190	
191	    await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id: course.id, overrideAccess: true })
192	
193	    // after delete: verify via a single batched query rather than N sequential reads
194	    // (Sequential per-user findByID calls exhaust the test DB connection pool.)
195	    const remaining = (await payload.find({
196	      collection: 'users',
197	      where: { id: { in: userIds }, 'courseEntitlements.course': { equals: course.id } },
198	      limit: 0,
199	      overrideAccess: true,
200	    })) as any
201	    expect(remaining.totalDocs).toBe(0)
202	  }, 180_000)
203	})

1	// @vitest-environment node
2	// Node.js environment required: payload.login() uses jose JWT signing which depends on
3	// Node.js's native TextEncoder/Uint8Array. The jsdom environment can cause a
4	// Uint8Array realm mismatch that breaks jose's FlattenedSign constructor check.

6	/**
7	 * Integration tests: Dashboard Metrics API — Course Enrollments (#1373)
8	 *
9	 * Verifies that the route returns multiple courses with correct enrollment
10	 * counts when users have entitlements to different courses. This guards
11	 * against the regression where ObjectId-shaped course refs collapsed to a
12	 * single bucket and only one course appeared in the response.
13	 *
14	 * Pattern: matches tests/int/teacher-profiles-api.int.spec.ts — uses the
15	 * shared Payload instance from global-int-setup, imports the route's GET
16	 * directly, and authenticates with `Authorization: JWT <token>`.
17	 */
18	/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
19	import { GET } from '@/app/api/admin/dashboard-metrics/route'
20	import { AccountRole } from '@/server/payload/collections/Users/roles'
21	import config from '@payload-config'
22	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
23	import { getPayload } from 'payload'
24	import { afterAll, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest'
25	
26	const hasDatabaseUrl = !!process.env.DATABASE_URL
27	
28	let payload: Payload
29	let adminToken: string
30	const createdUserIds: string[] = []
31	const createdCourseIds: string[] = []
32	let categoryId: string | undefined
33	let tenantId: string | undefined
34	
35	const ts = Date.now()
36	
37	beforeAll(async () => {
38	  if (!hasDatabaseUrl) return
39	
40	  payload = await getPayload({ config })
41	
42	  // Tenant + category needed by courses
43	  const tenant = await payload.create({
44	    collection: 'tenants',
45	    data: {
46	      name: `metrics-${ts}`,
47	      slug: `metrics-${ts}`,
48	      status: 'active',
49	    } as any,
50	    overrideAccess: true,
51	  })
52	  tenantId = tenant.id
53	
54	  const category = await payload.create({
55	    collection: 'categories',
56	    data: { title: 'Metrics Cat', slug: `metrics-cat-${ts}`, locale: 'he' } as any,
57	    overrideAccess: true,
58	  })
59	  categoryId = category.id
60	
61	  // Three distinct courses
62	  for (const i of [1, 2, 3]) {
63	    const c = await payload.create({
64	      collection: 'courses',
65	      data: {
66	        courseLabel: `Course ${i}`,
67	        title: `Test Course ${i} ${ts}`,
68	        categories: [categoryId],
69	        tenant: tenantId,
70	        status: 'published',
71	      } as any,
72	      overrideAccess: true,
73	    })
74	    createdCourseIds.push(c.id)
75	  }
76	  const [course1Id, course2Id, course3Id] = createdCourseIds
77	
78	  // Admin user used for auth — create as default user, then promote.
79	  // The Users collection has a beforeChange hook that prevents setting
80	  // role=Admin on initial create; only update with overrideAccess can.
81	  // See tests/int/access-codes.int.spec.ts for the canonical pattern.
82	  const adminEmail = `admin-metrics-${ts}@test.local`
83	  const adminPassword = 'test-password-1234'
84	  const adminUser = await payload.create({
85	    collection: 'users',
86	    data: {
87	      email: adminEmail,
88	      password: adminPassword,
89	      name: 'Metrics Admin',
90	    } as any,
91	  })
92	  await payload.update({
93	    collection: 'users',
94	    id: adminUser.id,
95	    data: { role: AccountRole.Admin } as any,
96	    overrideAccess: true,
97	  })
98	  createdUserIds.push(adminUser.id)
99	
100	  const login = await payload.login({
101	    collection: 'users',
102	    data: { email: adminEmail, password: adminPassword },
103	  })
104	  adminToken = login.token!
105	
106	  // Enrollments — Course1: 3 (u1,u2,u5), Course2: 2 (u3,u5), Course3: 1 (u4)
107	  const enrollmentSpecs: Array<{ email: string; courses: string[] }> = [
108	    { email: `u1-${ts}@test.local`, courses: [course1Id] },
109	    { email: `u2-${ts}@test.local`, courses: [course1Id] },
110	    { email: `u3-${ts}@test.local`, courses: [course2Id] },
111	    { email: `u4-${ts}@test.local`, courses: [course3Id] },
112	    { email: `u5-${ts}@test.local`, courses: [course1Id, course2Id] },
113	  ]
114	  for (const spec of enrollmentSpecs) {
115	    const u = await payload.create({
116	      collection: 'users',
117	      data: {
118	        email: spec.email,
119	        password: adminPassword,
120	        name: spec.email,
121	        courseEntitlements: spec.courses.map((c) => ({
122	          course: c,
123	          grantMethod: 'admin',
124	<<<<<<< HEAD
125	          transactionId: `txn-${c}`,
126	=======
127	          transactionId: 'test-transaction',
128	>>>>>>> origin/dev
129	        })),
130	      } as any,
131	      overrideAccess: true,
132	    })
133	    createdUserIds.push(u.id)
134	  }
135	}, 60_000)
136	
137	afterAll(async () => {
138	  if (!hasDatabaseUrl || !payload) return
139	
140	  for (const id of createdUserIds) {
141	    try {
142	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'users', id, overrideAccess: true })
143	    } catch {
144	      /* already deleted */
145	    }
146	  }
147	  for (const id of createdCourseIds) {
148	    try {
149	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'courses', id, overrideAccess: true })
150	    } catch {
151	      /* already deleted */
152	    }
153	  }
154	  if (categoryId) {
155	    try {
156	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'categories', id: categoryId, overrideAccess: true })
157	    } catch {
158	      /* already deleted */
159	    }
160	  }
161	  if (tenantId) {
162	    try {
163	      await payload.delete({ collection: 'tenants', id: tenantId, overrideAccess: true })
164	    } catch {
165	      /* already deleted */
166	    }
167	  }
168	}, 60_000)
169	
170	describe.skipIf(!hasDatabaseUrl)('GET /api/admin/dashboard-metrics — course enrollments', () => {
171	  it('returns 401 when not authenticated', async () => {
172	    const req = new Request('http://localhost:3000/api/admin/dashboard-metrics?period=month')
173	    const res = await GET(req)
174	    expect(res.status).toBe(401)
175	  })
176	
177	  it('aggregates multiple courses with correct counts', async () => {
178	    const req = new Request('http://localhost:3000/api/admin/dashboard-metrics?period=month', {
179	      headers: { Authorization: `JWT ${adminToken}` },
180	    })
181	    const res = await GET(req)
182	    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
183	
184	    const body = (await res.json()) as {
185	      engagement: { courseEnrollments: Array<{ courseTitle: string; count: number }> }
186	    }
187	    const titleToCount = new Map(
188	      body.engagement.courseEnrollments.map((e) => [e.courseTitle, e.count]),
189	    )
190	
191	    expect(titleToCount.get(`Test Course 1 ${ts}`)).toBe(3)
192	    expect(titleToCount.get(`Test Course 2 ${ts}`)).toBe(2)
193	    expect(titleToCount.get(`Test Course 3 ${ts}`)).toBe(1)
194	  })
195	})

1. **`.kody/last-run.jsonl`** - This is a session log file. The file contains 3 lines (entries), which appear to be from the current HEAD branch. The origin/dev version is in the "Working-tree conflict markers" section and shows a different session ID. Since this is a log file from a specific session, I should take the HEAD version (the current branch's session log), as it represents the actual state of this memorize PR.

2. **`tests/int/course-entitlement-cascade-delete.int.spec.ts`** - The conflict is at lines 79-83:
   - HEAD: `transactionId: \`txn-${id}\`` 
   - origin/dev: `transactionId: 'test-transaction'`
   
   Looking at the context, the function `createUserWithEntitlements` receives `courseIds: string[]` and maps over them to create entitlements. Using `txn-${id}` creates a unique transaction ID per course, which is more specific and appropriate for testing. The HEAD version is better.

3. **`tests/int/dashboard-metrics.int.spec.ts`** - The conflict is at lines 124-128:
   - HEAD: `transactionId: \`txn-${c}\``
   - origin/dev: `transactionId: 'test-transaction'`
   
   Same pattern - using the course ID `c` to create a unique transaction ID is better for testing. HEAD wins.

Let me resolve these conflicts now.

content.text:
Now I understand all three conflicts. Let me resolve them:
